section.heading: Inspector General external review panel

content.p: An individual with a claim described in subsection (b) may submit to the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community a request for a review of such claim by an external review panel convened under subsection (c).

subsection.heading: Claims and individuals described
subsection.chapeau: A claim described in this subsection is any—

subparagraph.content: that the individual has been subjected to a personnel action that is prohibited under section 3234 of this title; and
who has exhausted the applicable review process for the claim pursuant to enforcement of such section; or
that he or she has been subjected to a reprisal prohibited by paragraph (1) of section 3341(j) of this title; and
who received a decision on an appeal regarding that claim under paragraph (4) of such section.

content.p: Upon receipt of a request under subsection (a) regarding a claim, the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community may, at the discretion of the Inspector General, convene an external review panel under this subsection to review the claim.

subparagraph.heading: Composition
subparagraph.chapeau: An external review panel convened under this subsection shall be composed of three members as follows:

clause.content: The Inspector General of the Intelligence Community.

clause.chapeau: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), two members selected by the Inspector General as the Inspector General considers appropriate on a case-by-case basis from among inspectors general of the following:

content.p: An inspector general of an agency may not be selected to sit on the panel under subparagraph (A)(ii) to review any matter relating to a decision made by such agency.
Except as provided in clause (ii), the chairperson of any panel convened under this subsection shall be the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community.

clause.heading: Conflicts of interest
clause.chapeau: If the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community finds cause to recuse himself or herself from a panel convened under this subsection, the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community shall—

subclause.content: select a chairperson from inspectors general of the elements listed under subparagraph (A)(ii) whom the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community considers appropriate; and
notify the congressional intelligence committees of such selection.

content.p: Each external review panel convened under this subsection to review a claim shall complete review of the claim no later than 270 days after the date on which the Inspector General convenes the external review panel.

paragraph.heading: Panel recommendations
paragraph.chapeau: If an external review panel convened under subsection (c) determines, pursuant to a review of a claim submitted by an individual under subsection (a), that the individual was the subject of a personnel action prohibited under section 3234 of this title or was subjected to a reprisal prohibited by section 3341(j)(1) of this title, the panel may recommend that the agency head take corrective action—

subparagraph.chapeau: in the case of an employee or former employee—

clause.content: to return the employee or former employee, as nearly as practicable and reasonable, to the position such employee or former employee would have held had the reprisal not occurred; or
reconsider the employee’s or former employee’s eligibility for access to classified information consistent with national security; or

subparagraph.content: in any other case, such other action as the external review panel considers appropriate.

subparagraph.heading: In general
subparagraph.chapeau: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the head of an agency receives a recommendation from an external review panel under paragraph (1), the head shall—

clause.content: give full consideration to such recommendation; and
inform the panel and the Director of National Intelligence of what action the head has taken with respect to the recommendation.

content.p: The Director shall notify the President of any failures to comply with subparagraph (A)(ii).

Not less frequently than once each year, the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community shall submit to the congressional intelligence committees and the Director of National Intelligence a report on the activities under this section during the previous year.

paragraph.heading: Contents
paragraph.chapeau: Subject to such limitations as the Inspector General of the Intelligence Community considers necessary to protect the privacy of an individual who has made a claim described in subsection (b), each report submitted under paragraph (1) shall include, for the period covered by the report, the following:

subparagraph.content: The determinations and recommendations made by the external review panels convened under this section.
The responses of the heads of agencies that received recommendations from the external review panels.

section.sourceCredit: (July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title XI, § 1106, as added Pub. L. 116–92, div. E, title LIII, § 5332(a)(1), Dec. 20, 2019, 133 Stat. 2137.)
